sense.definition: a metal tool with a toothed blade that is used for cutting wood, metal, etc. by moving back and forth

examples.x: He used a saw to cut the wooden board to the desired length.
He carefully measured before using the saw to ensure accuracy.
The saw's sharp teeth helped it glide smoothly through the material.
She checked the blade of the saw for any signs of wear before starting.
He kept the saw in a secure place when not in use to prevent accidents.

sense.definition: a manual cutting tool with a toothed blade and a handle, used for making straight or curved cuts in various materials such as wood, plastic, or metal, typically operated by hand without the need for power

examples.x: He used a hand saw to cut the wooden boards for the new bookshelf.
The carpenter grabbed his hand saw to trim the edges of the door.
After measuring the wood, she carefully used a hand saw to make the cut.
They preferred using a hand saw for the project to keep things quiet in the workshop.

sense.definition: a type of hand saw with a wide, toothed blade designed for cutting wood perpendicular to the grain, producing smooth, clean crosscuts rather than ripping cuts along the grain

examples.x: He used a crosscut saw to trim the branches of the tree.
The carpenter grabbed the crosscut saw to cut through the wooden beam.
For a cleaner finish, always use a crosscut saw when cutting across the grain of the wood.
She carefully lined up the crosscut saw before starting the cut on the plank.
After measuring the length, he pulled out the crosscut saw to make the final cut.

sense.definition: a type of hand saw with a long, toothed blade specifically designed for cutting wood along the grain, making it efficient for ripping or cutting boards lengthwise with the grain for tasks like rough dimensioning or creating parallel edges

examples.x: The carpenter used a rip saw to cut the long wooden board along its grain.
For a smoother cut, he switched to the rip saw instead of a crosscut saw.
She grabbed the rip saw from the toolbox to trim the length of the lumber.
He needed a rip saw to cut the boards for the framework of the house.
After measuring the wood, they carefully used the rip saw to ensure the cuts were straight.

sense.definition: a type of hand saw with a reinforced back edge that provides stability and rigidity to the thin, fine-toothed blade

examples.x: I find that a back saw works better than a regular saw for making detailed, clean cuts.
For the fine woodworking project, I grabbed a back saw to ensure accurate miter cuts.
The carpenter used a back saw to make precise cuts for the furniture joints.
She reached for the back saw when she needed to cut the narrow piece of wood accurately.

sense.definition: a small, handheld saw with a narrow, fine-toothed blade stretched across a U-shaped frame, commonly used for intricate and detailed cuts in wood, such as coping moldings or making curved or irregular shapes

examples.x: He replaced the blade of the coping saw before continuing the delicate carving work.
The craftsman used a coping saw to trim the edges of the wooden door to fit perfectly in the frame.
The coping saw made it easy to shape the intricate design on the wooden frame.
With the coping saw, she was able to make precise cuts around the detailed edges of the mold.

sense.definition: a small hand saw with a narrow, pointed blade designed for making quick and rough cuts in drywall, plywood, or other similar materials

examples.x: He used a jab saw to cut a hole in the drywall for the new light switch.
She grabbed the jab saw to trim around the pipes in the bathroom wall.
The carpenter reached for the jab saw to carve out the shape for the air vent.
He carefully used the jab saw to create an opening in the wall for the wiring.

sense.definition: a handheld saw with a fine-toothed blade held under tension in a frame, commonly used for cutting metal, plastic, or other hard materials by pushing and pulling the saw blade back and forth

examples.x: He used a hacksaw to cut the metal pipe for the plumbing project.
She found the hacksaw to be the best tool for getting through the tough material.
After a few minutes of cutting, the hacksaw finally sliced through the thick plastic.
The mechanic grabbed the hacksaw to trim the metal rod to the right size.
To fix the fence, I had to use a hacksaw to shorten the metal brackets.

sense.definition: a large, coarse-toothed hand saw with a narrow, flexible blade held under tension in a curved frame, typically used for cutting through green wood or branches in gardening, landscaping, or woodworking tasks

examples.x: He used a bow saw to trim the overgrown branches in the backyard.
She reached for the bow saw when she needed to cut the logs into smaller pieces for the fire.
After a few hours of work with the bow saw, the pile of branches was finally cleared.
I borrowed a bow saw from the neighbor to help with cutting the fallen tree in the yard.

sense.definition: a specialized saw with a sharp-toothed blade designed for trimming and cutting branches or limbs from trees or shrubs

examples.x: He used the pruning saw to trim the overgrown branches of the tree.
The gardener reached for the pruning saw to cut off the dead wood.
She carefully worked with the pruning saw, making sure not to damage the healthy branches.
After a few minutes with the pruning saw, the large branch was finally cut free.
The arborist sharpened his pruning saw before starting the day's work on the trees.

sense.definition: a type of hand saw with a thin, flexible blade that cuts on the pull stroke, offering precise and smooth cuts in various woodworking tasks, and often characterized by its fine teeth and ergonomic handle design

examples.x: The carpenter used a Japanese saw to make the delicate cuts needed for the wooden joint.
When trimming the branches, he preferred using a Japanese saw for its smooth, clean cuts.
For fine woodworking, a Japanese saw provides more precision compared to a traditional hand saw.
She switched to a Japanese saw to get better control while working on her detailed project.
After struggling with the standard saw, he found that the Japanese saw was much easier to use for fine trim work.

sense.definition: a power tool used for making accurate crosscuts and miter cuts in wood, metal, or other materials, featuring a circular blade mounted on a hinged arm that can be pivoted to different angles for precise angled cuts

examples.x: The carpenter used a miter saw to cut the molding at the perfect angle for the corner.
To make the picture frame, he set the miter saw to a 45-degree angle.
She used the miter saw to trim the edges of the door frame, ensuring a clean, sharp cut.
After measuring the angle, he adjusted the miter saw and made a quick, clean cut through the wood.
For the best results, it is important to secure the wood firmly before using the miter saw.

sense.definition: a handheld power tool with a blade that moves back and forth in a rapid, reciprocating motion, commonly used for demolition work or making rough cuts in wood, metal, or other materials

examples.x: He used a reciprocating saw to quickly cut through the old wooden beams during the demolition.
The contractor grabbed the reciprocating saw to remove the damaged pipes under the sink.
She switched out the blade on the reciprocating saw to make a cleaner cut in the plastic pipes.
The demolition team relied on their reciprocating saws to tear down the walls efficiently.
The electrician used a reciprocating saw to trim the PVC piping for the new installation.

sense.definition: a power tool used for cutting tiles and ceramics, featuring a diamond-tipped blade and a water cooling system for clean and precise cuts

examples.x: The contractor used a tile saw to cut the tiles for the kitchen backsplash.
If you want to get clean edges on your tiles, a tile saw is the best tool to use.
I rented a tile saw from the hardware store to finish the bathroom renovation.
He carefully measured the tiles before using the tile saw to make sure they fit perfectly.

sense.definition: a hand tool with a small, narrow blade used for cutting thin veneer sheets or trimming veneer edges in woodworking projects

examples.x: The craftsman used a veneer saw to carefully trim the edges of the thin wood layer.
To avoid damaging the delicate surface, she opted for a veneer saw when cutting the veneer for her cabinet project.
After measuring the piece, he used the veneer saw to cut it to the exact size needed for the tabletop.
She relied on a veneer saw to achieve smooth and clean cuts on the fine wood veneer for the antique restoration.

sense.definition: a handheld tool with a narrow, pointed blade used for making small and intricate cuts in materials like drywall, wood, or plastic

examples.x: The carpenter used a keyhole saw to cut a small hole for the electrical outlet.
After marking the shape, she carefully used a keyhole saw to cut out the curve in the wood.
He grabbed the keyhole saw to trim the edges of the paneling where it met the wall.
The plumber used a keyhole saw to make a small cut in the wood to fit the pipe through.
She used a keyhole saw to carve a circular hole for the vent in the plasterboard.

sense.definition: a flexible hand saw designed for making flush cuts or trimming materials close to a surface

examples.x: The carpenter used a flush cut saw to trim the wooden dowels so they were level with the surface.
When installing the cabinet hardware, the builder relied on a flush cut saw to make precise cuts around the edges.
She grabbed the flush cut saw to carefully remove the nail sticking out of the doorframe.
After gluing the pieces together, the woodworker used the flush cut saw to clean up the joints.
He reached for the flush cut saw to cut the small bump from the surface of the tabletop before sanding it smooth.

sense.definition: a versatile power tool with a circular rotating blade, commonly used for making straight cuts in various materials such as wood, metal, or plastic

examples.x: He used the circular saw to cut through the thick piece of wood for his project.
After setting the fence, he used the circular saw to trim the edges of the board.
She carefully measured the wood before turning on the circular saw to ensure a straight cut.
The carpenter reached for the circular saw to finish cutting the beams for the house frame.
To speed up the process, they decided to use the circular saw instead of a hand saw.

sense.definition: a stationary power tool consisting of a flat table surface with a circular saw blade protruding through it, used for making precise cuts in wood or other materials by sliding the workpiece against the blade along a guide or fence

examples.x: The carpenter used a table saw to cut the plywood for the new cabinet.
After measuring the wood, she carefully fed it into the table saw to make precise cuts.
He used the table saw to rip the boards into smaller pieces for the project.
For this project, a table saw will give you the cleanest, most accurate cuts.

sense.definition: a handheld power tool equipped with a thin, reciprocating blade that moves up and down rapidly, used for making curved or intricate cuts in wood, metal, or other materials by guiding the blade along a desired cutting path
a stationary power tool with a fine, vertical reciprocating blade that allows for intricate and precise cutting in wood, plastic, or other materials

examples.x: The artisan used a scroll saw to create intricate patterns on the wooden panel.
She carefully adjusted the blade of the scroll saw to make curved cuts in the thin wood.
I prefer using a scroll saw for delicate work because it offers precision and control.
After hours of cutting, the scroll saw made the complex shapes needed for the model.
Using a scroll saw, the artist created delicate, lacy patterns on the piece of wood.

sense.definition: a cutting tool that uses a long, continuous blade with teeth along one edge to make straight or curved cuts in a variety of materials, such as wood, metal, or plastic

examples.x: The carpenter used a band saw to cut the wood into thinner pieces for the furniture project.
He carefully adjusted the band saw to make sure it could cut through the thick metal sheet.
She resawed the lumber using the band saw to create perfectly even planks.
The workshop had several band saws, each set up for different types of cuts.
They used the band saw to cut out the complex shapes needed for the wooden puzzle pieces.

sense.definition: a power tool equipped with a circular abrasive disc or blade used for cutting through metal, masonry, or other hard materials by grinding or abrasive action

examples.x: The worker used an abrasive saw to cut through the thick metal pipe.
For the construction project, an abrasive saw was necessary to slice through the concrete blocks.
To make the cut, the team employed an abrasive saw with a diamond-tipped blade.
When cutting hard stone tiles, an abrasive saw provides the precision needed.
The fabricator used an abrasive saw to cut the heavy-duty metal sheets into smaller sections.

sense.definition: a power tool equipped with a diamond or abrasive blade designed specifically for cutting through concrete, asphalt, or other hard materials

examples.x: The workers used a concrete saw to cut through the thick foundation for the new building.
They rented a concrete saw to break apart the old driveway before pouring the new one.
A diamond blade was fitted to the concrete saw to ensure a clean cut through the reinforced concrete.
The construction team used a large concrete saw to remove sections of the pavement for repair work.

sense.definition: a power tool with a circular saw blade mounted on a guide rail or track, enabling straight and precise cuts in various materials

examples.x: For his woodworking project, John preferred the track saw because it was easier to handle on large sheets of material.
He used a track saw to ensure perfectly straight cuts when installing the new flooring.
She set up the track saw along the edge of the board and made a clean, straight cut.
I bought a track saw to help with my home renovation, as it’s more portable than a table saw.

sense.definition: a specialized tool for cutting insulation materials, featuring a serrated blade designed for clean cuts

examples.x: I need to grab the insulation saw to cut these pieces of mineral wool for the new house.
He used an insulation saw to trim the fiberglass insulation to fit the wall cavities perfectly.
She reached for the insulation saw to make a quick, clean cut in the thick fiberglass batts.
With an insulation saw, the contractor quickly cut the batts of insulation to fit the tight spaces.

sense.definition: a hand saw consisting of a thin, narrow blade stretched across a frame, commonly used in woodworking and crafts for making intricate curved cuts, particularly in thin materials like wood, plastic, or metal

examples.x: She carefully guided the fretsaw through the curve to ensure a smooth, precise cut.
The artist preferred using a fretsaw for her custom wood designs, as it allowed for greater detail.
After measuring the pattern, he picked up the fretsaw to trim the excess wood.
For the fine details of the model, the designer chose a fretsaw over other tools for its precision.

sense.definition: a hand saw with a narrow blade and a reinforced back, used for making precise cuts in woodworking, particularly for cutting tenons or making accurate crosscuts

examples.x: The carpenter used a tenon saw to make clean, straight cuts on the wood for the door frame.
He carefully measured the pieces before using the tenon saw to ensure the joints would fit perfectly.
After marking the lines, she picked up the tenon saw to cut the wood accurately.
For the furniture project, a tenon saw was essential to create precise joints for the legs and rails.
Using a tenon saw, the woodworker cut the tenons perfectly to ensure a strong, secure fit.

sense.definition: a hand saw with a narrow, fine-toothed blade and a distinctive shape that resembles a compass, hence the name

examples.x: He used a compass saw to cut out the intricate curves on the wooden frame.
For this project, a compass saw was the best tool to cut the curved shape of the door.
The carpenter relied on a compass saw to carefully carve the wooden design.
I grabbed the compass saw to finish the detailed cuts around the window.

sense.definition: a small, fine-toothed hand saw specifically designed for making precise and delicate cuts, particularly in woodworking tasks such as cutting dovetail joints or other intricate joinery

examples.x: The carpenter used a dovetail saw to create the intricate joints for the wooden drawer.
She carefully guided the dovetail saw along the marked lines to ensure the cuts were precise.
To assemble the antique cabinet, he relied on a dovetail saw to make the tight-fitting corners.
The woodworker chose a dovetail saw over a regular saw for the detailed cuts required in the project.
After marking the wood, he carefully used the dovetail saw to create perfect, interlocking joints.

sense.definition: a tool specifically designed to cut pipes with accuracy and efficiency

examples.x: The plumber used a pipe cutter to trim the copper pipe to the correct length.
She grabbed the pipe cutter to fix the leaking section of the water supply.
The technician carefully measured and marked the pipe before using the pipe cutter.
I need to buy a pipe cutter before I start installing the new plumbing system.

sense.definition: a tool used to create threads on the ends of pipes to facilitate the connection and joining of pipes and fittings

examples.x: The plumber used a pipe threader to add threads to the metal pipes before connecting them to the fittings.
To complete the installation, he needed a pipe threader to thread the ends of the copper pipes.
The mechanic used a manual pipe threader to create threads on the pipes for the new water system.
The mechanic used a manual pipe threader to create threads on the pipes for the new water system.
When working with larger pipes, a powered pipe threader can speed up the threading process.

sense.definition: a hand tool with a pointed end on one side and a flat chisel-like blade on the other, used for breaking up hard surfaces such as rocks, soil, or concrete

examples.x: The workers used a pickaxe to break up the tough soil for the foundation.
After hours of digging with a pickaxe, they finally hit a large rock.
He swung the pickaxe with all his strength, trying to break through the concrete.
The miner used a pickaxe to extract the ore from the hard rock.
She picked up the pickaxe and began clearing the garden of stubborn roots.

sense.definition: a tool that combines an axe-like blade on one side and an adze-like digging tool on the other

examples.x: The crew worked together, using their Pulaskis to remove brush and create a firebreak.
The park ranger demonstrated how to properly use a Pulaski when clearing debris from the hiking path.

sense.definition: a heavy, axe-like tool with a large, wedge-shaped head designed for splitting wood along the grain by delivering powerful blows

examples.x: He used the splitting maul to break the logs into smaller pieces for the firewood pile.
After hours of work, the splitting maul was covered in wood chips and sawdust.
She picked up the splitting maul and swung it with precision, splitting the log in one hit.
They used the splitting maul to prepare enough firewood for the winter season.
The sound of the splitting maul hitting the log echoed through the forest.

sense.definition: a long and rigid metal bar that is used in conjunction with socket wrenches or other tools to provide additional leverage for breaking stubborn or rusted bolts, nuts, or fasteners

examples.x: The mechanic used a breaker bar to loosen the rusted bolt on the car's exhaust system.
I could not remove the tight nut until I grabbed the breaker bar for extra leverage.
For heavy-duty work, a breaker bar is often the best tool to break free stuck fasteners.
He applied steady pressure with the breaker bar, finally loosening the wheel lug nut.
The workers used a breaker bar to loosen the bolts on the construction site machinery.

sense.definition: a hand tool with a sharp, hardened steel blade used for cutting, shaping, or chipping away hard materials such as metal, stone, or concrete

examples.x: He used a cold chisel to remove the rusted bolt from the engine.
The worker carefully struck the cold chisel to break the concrete slab into smaller pieces.
She reached for the cold chisel to carve the stone into a smooth surface.
The blacksmith used a cold chisel to shape the metal into the desired form.
After several attempts, the mechanic finally loosened the stuck bolt with a cold chisel.

sense.definition: a wedge-shaped tool used with a hammer or mallet to split logs or wood by driving it into the material to create a clean split along the grain

examples.x: The lumberjack used a splitting wedge to break the large log into smaller pieces for firewood.
He struck the splitting wedge with a heavy hammer, and the wood cracked open easily.
The splitting wedge worked well on the tough oak, allowing the workers to separate the logs.
After several strikes with the splitting wedge, the stone finally broke into manageable pieces.
The men gathered around the fire, using a splitting wedge to prepare the firewood for the night.

sense.definition: a technique used in stone splitting where a series of holes are drilled into the stone, and then pairs of metal wedges (feathers) and a center wedge (plug) are inserted

examples.x: Using a plug and feather method, the stone was split with precision along the natural crack.
The workers used the plug and feather technique to break the large boulder into smaller pieces.
He carefully set the plug and feather into the rock to ensure the split would go in the right direction.
The mason used the plug and feather method to split the large slab of granite into thinner sections.

sense.definition: a power tool used in woodworking to create biscuit joints by cutting slots in the edges of two pieces of wood, allowing for precise and strong joining with the help of inserted biscuits

examples.x: The woodworker used a biscuit jointer to ensure the table top pieces were aligned perfectly before gluing them together.
He carefully adjusted the depth of the blade on the biscuit jointer to match the size of the biscuits.
With the biscuit jointer, the carpenter was able to create precise cuts for the shelves without much effort.
After using the biscuit jointer, the pieces of wood fit together smoothly and securely.

sense.definition: a tool designed to cleanly and efficiently cut through various types of cables, such as electrical cables, wire ropes, or coaxial cables

examples.x: He used a cable cutter to trim the excess wire after installing the new light fixture.
The electrician grabbed the cable cutter to snip the thick power cables quickly.
For the home renovation project, she needed a cable cutter to shorten the electrical wires.
The technician carefully used the cable cutter to avoid damaging the insulation on the wires.
After pulling the wires through the conduit, she used a cable cutter to cut them to the right size.

sense.definition: a specialized tool designed for electricians, featuring a sharp blade and insulated handle, used for cutting and stripping insulation from electrical wires and cables
a tool used to create precise holes in metal surfaces, typically in electrical enclosures or panels, to accommodate conduit or pipe fittings
a hand tool used for removing the insulation or outer covering from electrical wires with precision and without damaging the wire itself

examples.x: The electrician used a wire stripper to carefully remove the insulation before connecting the wires.
She showed the apprentice how to properly use a wire stripper for clean, damage-free stripping.
He reached for his wire stripper to prepare the cables for the new light fixture.
The toolset included a wire stripper for working on various electrical projects.

sense.definition: a handheld tool used to tighten and cut cable ties or zip ties

examples.x: The technician used a cable tie gun to neatly organize the wires inside the control panel.
He grabbed the cable tie gun to secure the bundle of cords along the wall.
For a clean finish, always use a cable tie gun to cut the excess tie after tightening.
She demonstrated how to use the cable tie gun to fasten the wires securely without over-tightening.

sense.definition: a type of pliers with sharp, angled jaws designed for cutting wire and small materials in electrical and general applications

examples.x: The electrician used diagonal pliers to cut the wires neatly during the installation.
She grabbed her diagonal pliers to remove the old staples from the wood panel.
When working on jewelry, diagonal pliers are great for cutting thin metal wires.
He used the diagonal pliers to clip the zip ties holding the cables together.

sense.definition: a small, single-handed axe with a short handle, typically used for chopping wood or small-scale cutting tasks

a versatile cutting tool with a retractable blade, commonly used for various tasks such as cutting materials like cardboard, plastic, ropes, or opening packages in both professional and household settings

examples.x: He carefully used the utility knife to trim the edges of the paper.
She always keeps a utility knife in her toolbox for various household repairs.
Make sure to replace the blade in the utility knife when it starts to dull.
He used a utility knife to cut through the packaging before opening the box.
The utility knife was the best tool for cutting the thin plastic sheeting.

sense.definition: a cutting tool with multiple cutting edges used to enlarge and refine the diameter of an existing hole with precision and smoothness
a specialized plumbing tool designed to remove burrs and smooth the inner edges of a pipe or tube after cutting or threading

examples.x: The plumber used a pipe reamer to clean the edges of the cut pipe before installing the fitting.
After cutting the copper pipe, he grabbed the pipe reamer to smooth out the inside edges.
He carefully rotated the pipe reamer to remove any burrs left after cutting the pipe.
When installing new pipes, always use a pipe reamer to ensure the edges are smooth and clean.

sense.definition: a power tool used in woodworking to hollow out an area or create intricate cuts by spinning a cutting bit at high speeds
tools used for cutting threads, with taps used for creating internal threads and dies used for creating external threads

examples.x: He used a tap and die set to create threads on the metal rod for the new project.
She needed a tap and die to make sure the screws fit properly into the machine.
The mechanic grabbed the tap and die set to repair the damaged threads on the bolt.
The worker carefully used the tap and die to create uniform threads on the pipe.

sense.definition: a hand tool with long handles and hardened steel jaws designed to cut through various types of metal objects, such as bolts, chains, wires, and padlocks

examples.x: He used the bolt cutter to remove the rusty chain from the fence.
The maintenance worker grabbed a pair of bolt cutters to fix the broken lock.
She needed a bolt cutter to cut through the thick wire before she could finish the repair.
The thief attempted to use a bolt cutter to break into the storage unit.
To complete the job, the contractor used a bolt cutter to trim the metal rods to the right size.

sense.definition: a tool that uses a very hot flame to cut metal or other hard materials by melting and removing them with a jet of oxygen
a tool with a toothed chain inside that is powered by a small engine, used for cutting wood

examples.x: He used a chainsaw to cut down the old tree in his yard.
The chainsaw's powerful motor made quick work of the thick logs.
She wore protective gear while using the chainsaw for safety.
The chainsaw came with safety features to prevent accidents.
He maintained the chainsaw by regularly sharpening the chain.

sense.definition: a tool with a long wooden handle attached to a heavy steel or iron blade, primarily used for chopping wood and cutting down trees

examples.x: He used an ax to chop firewood for the winter.
The ax had a sharp blade that made cutting easier.
She learned how to handle an ax safely during the camping trip.
The lumberjack carried his ax as he worked in the forest.
He polished the wooden handle of his grandfather's old ax.
